title: Spermacoce litoralis
intro: Spermacoce litoralis, also known as Beach Widow's Thrill or Beach Spermacoce, is a flowering plant species within the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and sprawling growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, where it is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce litoralis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Spermacoce litoralis is commonly used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Spermacoce litoralis belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Spermacoce litoralis is a species of flowering plant and its classification also includes being part of the genus Spermacoce. The classification of this plant at the kingdom level is Plantae, indicating it is a plant. Further classification includes being part of the clade Angiosperms, which are flowering plants.
uses: Spermacoce litoralis, a plant native to coastal regions, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is believed to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Spermacoce litoralis is used to treat digestive problems and is believed to have antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and their effectiveness in treating various health conditions.
